name: Return of Stack Variable Address
description: A function returns the address of a stack-allocated local variable, creating a dangling pointer. Subsequent function calls may overwrite that stack location, leading to crashes, memory corruption, or potential code execution.
detection_pattern: CONTROL node (function) returns a pointer from STORAGE (stack-allocated variable) via EGRESS. The returned address becomes invalid after the function's stack frame is deallocated, creating a dangling reference used by the caller.
